name: Tajik Soviet Socialist Republic (1938–1940)

colors.symbolism: the hammer and sickle and the inscription

description: A red field with a gold hammer and sickle in the upper hoist, beneath which the name of the republic is inscribed in gold in both the Latin Tajik script ('RSS Toçikiston') and Cyrillic.
history: Used 1938–1940, after the Tajik legend was Russified from 'ÇSS Toçikiston' to 'RSS Toçikiston'. It carried both the Latin Tajik inscription and a Cyrillic one, before the Latin script was dropped entirely when Tajik adopted Cyrillic on 28 September 1940.

features.symbolism: the union of industrial workers and peasants

features.role: Latin Tajik inscription 'RSS Toçikiston' with Cyrillic
features.symbolism: the name of the Tajik Soviet Socialist Republic
